description: VibeFinder 2.0 RAG knowledge base: genre profiles, activity contexts, and mood mappings used to enrich natural language query parsing.

documents.title: Gym / Workout / Exercise
documents.content: Working out at the gym, weightlifting, running, or any intense physical exercise requires music with very high energy (0.80-1.00). Ideal moods are intense, energetic, or angry to match physical effort. Electronic beats, heavy guitars, and pounding EDM work best. Acoustic instruments should be avoided. Fast tempo (130+ BPM) keeps pace. Best genres: edm, metal, rock, hip-hop, pop. Not suitable: classical, lofi, ambient, folk, jazz.

documents.title: Studying / Focus / Concentration
documents.content: Studying, focusing, coding, reading, or deep cognitive work benefits from low-to-medium energy music (0.30-0.50) that does not distract. Ideal moods: focused or chill. Acoustic textures are preferred for warmth without overload. Instrumental tracks or tracks with minimal vocals prevent lyrical interference. Best genres: lofi, ambient, classical, jazz. Popularity tends to be lower. Tempo: 60-90 BPM.

documents.title: Party / Social Gathering / Dancing
documents.content: Parties, social gatherings, and dancing require high-energy music (0.75-0.97) that is crowd-friendly and mainstream. Ideal moods: happy, energetic, or confident. Low acousticness (electronic production preferred). High popularity (70-100) ensures guests recognize the tracks. Fast tempo, strong beat. Best genres: pop, edm, hip-hop, reggae. Not suitable: metal, classical, ambient, folk.

documents.title: Driving / Road Trip
documents.content: Driving and road trips suit mid-to-high energy music (0.55-0.85) with a strong rhythmic pulse. Moods: energetic, nostalgic, or confident. Both acoustic and electronic work well. Genres: rock, country, pop, hip-hop, synthwave. Long-form listening means variety is appreciated, making diversity re-ranking useful.

documents.title: Sleeping / Bedtime / Wind Down
documents.content: Falling asleep or winding down requires very low energy music (0.10-0.30). Ideal moods: peaceful or relaxed. High acousticness preferred (0.70-1.00). Slow tempo, minimal percussion, no sudden dynamics. Best genres: classical, ambient, folk, lofi. Popularity is secondary; quality of calm matters most.

documents.title: Coffee Shop / Cafe / Daytime Background
documents.content: Coffee shop or cafe atmospheres benefit from low-to-medium energy music (0.30-0.55) with a relaxed, intelligent feel. Moods: relaxed, chill, or focused. Moderate acousticness. Genres: jazz, lofi, indie pop, folk. Tempo: 70-100 BPM. Not too loud or distracting.

documents.title: Meditation / Yoga / Mindfulness
documents.content: Meditation and yoga require the lowest energy levels (0.10-0.25). Mood: peaceful. Very high acousticness (0.80-1.00). Slow, minimal, drone-like or classical instrumentation. Genres: classical, ambient. No lyrics. Tempo: 50-70 BPM. The goal is to fade into the background without creating emotional distraction.

documents.title: Sad / Heartbreak / Emotional Release
documents.content: Feeling sad, going through heartbreak, or wanting an emotional release calls for melancholic or moody music. Energy range: 0.20-0.50. Moods: melancholic, moody, or nostalgic. Acoustic instruments are commonly preferred for authenticity. Genres: indie pop, folk, r&b, country, classical. Low energy and high acousticness make the music feel intimate.

documents.title: Morning Routine / Wake Up
documents.content: Morning routines benefit from moderate energy music (0.50-0.75) that gradually energizes without overwhelming. Moods: happy, energetic, or confident. Moderate acousticness is fine. Genres: pop, indie pop, r&b, reggae. A positive and uplifting feel helps start the day right. Tempo: 90-120 BPM.

documents.title: Late Night / Introspective / Moody
documents.content: Late-night listening tends toward introspective, atmospheric music with medium energy (0.40-0.70). Moods: moody, nostalgic, or chill. Both acoustic and electronic work. Genres: synthwave, r&b, jazz, lofi, indie pop. Lyrics that tell stories resonate well. Tempo: 75-110 BPM.

documents.title: Romantic / Date Night
documents.content: Romantic settings call for warm, intimate music with medium energy (0.45-0.70). Mood: romantic or peaceful. Moderate-to-high acousticness. Genres: r&b, jazz, indie pop, folk. High valence (positivity) helps create warmth. Smooth vocals and melodic instrumentation set the tone. Tempo: 75-100 BPM.

documents.title: Pop Music Genre
documents.content: Pop music is characterized by catchy melodies, polished production, and mainstream appeal. Energy: 0.70-0.95 for upbeat tracks, 0.40-0.65 for ballads. Moods: happy, energetic, romantic. Very high popularity (75-95). Low acousticness (0.05-0.35). Good for parties, driving, and casual listening. Tempo: 100-135 BPM.

documents.title: Lo-Fi Hip-Hop Genre
documents.content: Lo-fi hip-hop features low-fidelity audio, jazzy chord samples, soft drums, and a warm, nostalgic aesthetic. Energy: 0.30-0.50. Moods: chill, focused, or relaxed. High acousticness (0.60-0.90). Often instrumental or minimal vocals. Popularity tends to be moderate. Beloved for studying and focus sessions. Tempo: 70-90 BPM.

documents.title: Rock Music Genre
documents.content: Rock music is driven by electric guitars, strong drum beats, and powerful vocals. Energy: 0.70-0.95. Moods: intense, angry, or energetic. Low acousticness (0.05-0.25). Moderate-to-high popularity. Subgenres range from classic rock to modern alternative. Tempo: 110-168 BPM. Good for working out, driving, and emotional release.

documents.title: Jazz Music Genre
documents.content: Jazz is defined by improvisation, complex harmonies, and acoustic instrumentation. Energy: 0.30-0.65. Moods: relaxed, moody, or focused. High acousticness (0.60-0.95). Moderate popularity. Subgenres range from cool jazz to bebop. Tempo: 70-120 BPM. Excellent for coffee shops, late nights, and intellectual listening.

documents.title: Ambient / Atmospheric Genre
documents.content: Ambient music prioritizes texture and atmosphere over melody and rhythm. Energy: 0.15-0.40. Moods: peaceful, chill, or relaxed. Very high acousticness (0.75-0.98). Lower popularity. Minimal or absent percussion. Good for sleeping, meditation, and deep focus. Tempo: 50-80 BPM or arrhythmic.

documents.title: Synthwave Genre
documents.content: Synthwave is a retro-futuristic genre inspired by 1980s electronic music. Energy: 0.60-0.85. Moods: moody, nostalgic, or energetic. Very low acousticness (0.05-0.25). Driving synth arpeggios, pulsing bass, and cinematic pads. Moderate popularity. Good for driving at night, gaming, and working late. Tempo: 100-130 BPM.

documents.title: Indie Pop Genre
documents.content: Indie pop blends catchy pop songwriting with independent production aesthetics. Energy: 0.55-0.80. Moods: happy, nostalgic, or romantic. Moderate acousticness (0.25-0.55). Moderate popularity (65-82). Good for casual listening, morning routines, and social settings. Tempo: 100-130 BPM.

documents.title: Hip-Hop Genre
documents.content: Hip-hop centers on rhythmic vocal delivery (rap) over produced beats. Energy: 0.65-0.95. Moods: confident, energetic, or moody. Low acousticness (0.05-0.20). High popularity (80-95). Strong beat and bass. Good for workouts, confidence boosts, and parties. Tempo: 80-120 BPM.

documents.title: Classical Music Genre
documents.content: Classical music encompasses orchestral, chamber, and solo instrumental compositions. Energy: 0.15-0.45. Moods: peaceful, focused, or nostalgic. Very high acousticness (0.85-1.00). Lower popularity. Dynamic range from pianissimo to fortissimo. Great for studying, meditation, and relaxation. Tempo varies widely.

documents.title: R&B Genre
documents.content: R&B (rhythm and blues) blends soulful vocals with groovy production. Energy: 0.50-0.80. Moods: romantic, confident, or moody. Moderate acousticness (0.30-0.55). High popularity (65-85). Smooth, sensual production. Great for romantic evenings, late nights, and cruising. Tempo: 75-110 BPM.

documents.title: Country Music Genre
documents.content: Country music features storytelling lyrics, acoustic guitars, and American folk influences. Energy: 0.40-0.70. Moods: nostalgic, relaxed, or happy. High acousticness (0.60-0.85). Moderate popularity (58-75). Themes of home, heartbreak, and open roads. Good for driving, unwinding, and road trips. Tempo: 90-120 BPM.

documents.title: Metal Music Genre
documents.content: Metal is defined by heavily distorted guitars, fast tempos, and aggressive dynamics. Energy: 0.90-1.00. Moods: angry or intense. Very low acousticness (0.02-0.10). Moderate popularity. High BPM (150-200+). Best for extreme workouts, emotional release of aggression, and fans of heavy music. Not suitable for background or social settings.

documents.title: Reggae Genre
documents.content: Reggae features offbeat rhythms, bass-heavy production, and a laid-back groove. Energy: 0.45-0.70. Moods: peaceful, relaxed, or happy. Moderate acousticness (0.45-0.70). Moderate popularity (55-70). A positive, feel-good vibe. Good for relaxing, beach settings, and social occasions. Tempo: 70-100 BPM.

documents.title: EDM (Electronic Dance Music) Genre
documents.content: EDM encompasses house, techno, dubstep, and trance -- all high-energy electronic dance music. Energy: 0.85-1.00. Moods: energetic or happy. Very low acousticness (0.01-0.10). Very high popularity (85-95). Heavy bass, 4-on-the-floor kick drums, and synthesizer leads. Best for parties, clubs, and intense workouts. Tempo: 128-160 BPM.

documents.title: Folk Music Genre
documents.content: Folk music centers on acoustic instruments, storytelling, and rootsy tradition. Energy: 0.25-0.50. Moods: nostalgic, melancholic, or relaxed. Very high acousticness (0.75-0.95). Lower popularity (38-55). Acoustic guitar, banjo, fiddle. Excellent for quiet evenings, sad moods, and introspective listening. Tempo: 70-100 BPM.

documents.title: Happy / Joyful / Upbeat Mood
documents.content: Happy music conveys joy, positivity, and elation. High valence (0.70-0.95). Energy: 0.65-0.90. Synonyms: upbeat, cheerful, joyful, bright, fun, feel-good, sunny. Associated genres: pop, indie pop, reggae. Associated activities: parties, morning routines, celebrations.

documents.title: Chill / Laid-Back Mood
documents.content: Chill music is relaxed and unhurried without being sleepy. Energy: 0.30-0.55. Synonyms: mellow, laid-back, easy-going, breezy, casual, smooth. High acousticness often preferred. Associated genres: lofi, ambient, reggae, jazz. Good for studying, coffee shop, and relaxing.

documents.title: Melancholic / Sad / Blue Mood
documents.content: Melancholic music evokes sadness, longing, or introspection. Low valence (0.20-0.45). Energy: 0.20-0.45. Synonyms: sad, sorrowful, blue, gloomy, wistful, heartbroken, tearful, longing. Associated genres: folk, indie pop, classical, r&b. Associated activities: heartbreak, rainy days, emotional release.

documents.title: Focused / Concentrated Mood
documents.content: Focused music supports concentration and cognitive flow. Energy: 0.30-0.50. Often instrumental. Synonyms: concentrated, productive, alert, in-the-zone, studious. Associated genres: lofi, classical, ambient, jazz. Associated activities: studying, coding, deep work.

documents.title: Intense / Powerful Mood
documents.content: Intense music is forceful, aggressive, and emotionally overwhelming. High energy (0.80-1.00). Synonyms: powerful, fierce, aggressive, heavy, driving, hard, raw. Associated genres: rock, metal, edm. Associated activities: gym workouts, competition, emotional catharsis.

documents.title: Peaceful / Serene / Tranquil Mood
documents.content: Peaceful music creates a sense of calm and safety. Very low energy (0.10-0.35). Synonyms: serene, tranquil, calming, gentle, soothing, quiet. High acousticness. Associated genres: classical, ambient, folk. Associated activities: meditation, sleeping, unwinding.

documents.title: Nostalgic / Wistful / Sentimental Mood
documents.content: Nostalgic music evokes memories of the past and a longing for earlier times. Medium energy (0.40-0.65). Synonyms: sentimental, wistful, reminiscent, retro, throwback, vintage. Associated genres: folk, country, indie pop, synthwave. Decade preferences often matter here.

documents.title: Focus + Lo-Fi Study Session
documents.content: The most popular studying combination: lofi hip-hop with focused or chill mood. Energy: 0.35-0.45. High acousticness. Moderate popularity (45-65). This is the defining use case for lofi music. Query synonyms: study beats, lo-fi hip hop, background music for studying, concentration music.

documents.title: High-Intensity Workout + EDM
documents.content: The gym staple: EDM or metal with intense or energetic mood. Energy: 0.90-1.00. Very low acousticness. Very high tempo. Query synonyms: gym playlist, workout bangers, pump-up music, training music, pre-workout music, hype songs.

documents.title: Sad + Acoustic / Heartbreak Music
documents.content: Emotional listening after a breakup or on sad days: folk or indie pop with melancholic mood. Energy: 0.25-0.40. High acousticness. Low energy. Query synonyms: breakup songs, sad music, cry playlist, rainy day music, heartbreak songs, songs to cry to.

documents.title: Chill Jazz / Coffee Shop Ambience
documents.content: Jazz or lofi with a relaxed or chill mood for cafe settings. Energy: 0.35-0.55. High acousticness. Moderate popularity. Query synonyms: coffee shop music, cafe jazz, background jazz, study cafe, work from home music, jazz coffee.
